Trans-Neptunian Objects.CatText: Trans-Neptunian Objects (TNOs) are celestial bodies that orbit the Sun at a greater distance than Neptune. Many TNOs are classified as dwarf planets.

2003_UZ_413.Text: 2003 UZ413 is a Kuiper belt object that orbits the Sun beyond Neptune. It has a relatively circular orbit and is thought to be composed of a mixture of rock and ice. 2003 UZ413's surface is believed to be covered in a layer of complex organic compounds, giving it a reddish color.  It's very likely a dwarf planet but isn't currently classified as such pending confirmation.
Factoids: Has a very high density compared with other Kuiper belt objects.
It was discovered in 2003.
Has a rapid rotation period of approximately 4.13 hours.

2003_VS_2.Text: 2003 VS2 is Kuiper belt object, which like Pluto, orbits the sun in a 3:2 resonance with Neptune. It has a relatively bright surface, suggesting it may be covered in a layer of ices. 2003 VS2's orbit is less eccentric and inclined than Pluto however.
Factoids: Likely has an ellipsoid shape
Has a reddish color.

Achlys.Text: Achlys is a distant trans-Neptunian object discovered in 2015. It is one of the most distant known objects in the Solar System, with an orbit that takes it far beyond the Kuiper Belt into the inner Oort Cloud.
Factoids: Discovered in 2015.
Has an extremely elongated orbit that takes about 4,000 years to complete.
Named after the Greek goddess of mist and darkness.

Actaea.Text: Actaea is a satellite moon of the Dwarf Planet Salacia.

Altjira.Text: Altjira is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Arrokoth.Text: Arrokoth is a contact binary Kuiper belt object that was visited by the New Horizons spacecraft in 2019. It is composed of two lobes that are thought to have gently collided and stuck together in the early Solar System. Its surface is covered in a mixture of ices and organic compounds, giving it a reddish color.
Factoids: Arrokoth has a bilobed shape resembling a snowman.
It was discovered in 2014 using the Hubble Space Telescope.
Named 'Arrokoth', meaning 'sky' in the Powhatan/Algonquian language, in 2020.
Has a very low density, suggesting it is composed mostly of ice with some rock.
Provides insights into the conditions of the early Solar System and the formation of planetesimals.

Borasisi.Text: Borasisi is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Chaos.Text: Chaos is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Deucalion.Text: Deucalion is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Dysnomia.Text: Dysnomia is the only known moon of the dwarf planet Eris. It was discovered in 2005.
Factoids: Discovered in 2005.

Eris.Text: Eris is the most massive dwarf planet in the Solar System and was the discovery that triggered Pluto's reclassification. It has one known moon, Dysnomia. Eris has a highly elliptical orbit that takes it far beyond Pluto at its aphelion. Its surface is extremely cold and covered in a layer of methane ice, giving it a bright appearance.
Factoids: Discovered in 2005.
Named after the Greek goddess of strife and discord.
Has a highly elliptical orbit that takes about 558 years to complete.
Has one known moon, Dysnomia.

Gonggong.Text: Gonggong is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Haumea.Text: Haumea is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt known for its elongated shape and rapid rotation. It has two known moons.  Haumea's surface is primarily composed of crystalline water ice, giving it a bright appearance. Its rapid rotation causes it to have an oblong shape, making it one of the most unusual objects in the Solar System.
Factoids: Has a rapid rotation period of about 3.9 hours, which contributes to its elongated shape.
Has two known moons: Hiaka and Namaka.
Discovered in 2004.
Named after the Hawaiian goddess of fertility and childbirth.
One of the fastest rotating large objects in the Solar System.

Hiaka.Text: Hiaka is a small satellite of Haumea.
Factoids: Hiaka has a diameter of approximately 4 kilometers.
It was discovered in 2000.
Named after a Hawaiian goddess associated with dance and music.
Has a relatively bright surface, suggesting it may be covered in water ice.
Takes about 49 days to complete one orbit around Haumea.

Hiisi.Text: Hiisi is one of the two moons of the trans-Neptunian object Lempo.
Factoids: Discovered in 2001.
Has a diameter of approximately 100 kilometers.

Huya.Text: Huya is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Ixion.Text: Ixion is a large trans-Neptunian object and possible dwarf planet in the Kuiper belt.
Factoids: Discovered in 2001.
Named after a figure from Greek mythology who was punished to eternally spin on a fiery wheel.

Lempo.Text: Lempo is a triple system in the Kuiper Belt, consisting of a primary body and two smaller moons, Hiisi and Paha.
Factoids: Discovered in 2001.
The primary body is approximately 272 kilometers in diameter.
Hiisi and Paha are much smaller, with diameters of about 100 kilometers and 80 kilometers respectively.
Named after figures from Finnish mythology.

Logos.Text: Logos is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Makemake.Text: Makemake is the second brightest dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt after Pluto. It has one known moon.
Factoids: Discovered in 2005
Named after a deity of the Rapa Nui people of Easter Island.
Has a bright surface primarily composed of methane ice.
Takes about 305 years to complete one orbit around the Sun.
Has a very thin atmosphere that may form when it is closest to the Sun.
One of the largest known objects in the Kuiper Belt.
Its moon, S/2015 (MK2), was discovered in 2015.

MakemakeComet.Text: Makemake is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper belt that has a small moon named MK2. The moon was discovered in 2016 and is thought to be composed of water ice. MK2's orbit around Makemake is not well understood, but it is believed to be relatively close to the dwarf planet.
Factoids: MK2 is much smaller than Makemake, with an estimated diameter of around 175 kilometers.
The discovery of MK2 provided insights into the formation and evolution of the Makemake system.

Manwe.Text: Manwe is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Namaka.Text: Namaka is a small satellite of Haumea.
Factoids: Namaka has a diameter of approximately 5 kilometers.
It was discovered in 2001.
Named after a Hawaiian goddess associated with the sea.
Has a darker surface compared to Hiaka, indicating possible differences in composition or surface age.
Takes about 18 days to complete one orbit around Haumea.

Orcus.Text: Orcus is a large trans-Neptunian object and a possible dwarf planet in the Kuiper belt, orbiting the Sun at an average distance of 39.2 AU.
Factoids: Orcus has a large moon named Vanth.
It has a diameter of approximately 910 kilometers.
Discovered in 2004.
Orcus is sometimes referred to as the 'anti-Pluto' due to its similar orbit but opposite phase.

Paha.Text: Paha is one of the two moons of the trans-Neptunian object Lempo.
Factoids: Discovered in 2001.
Has a diameter of approximately 80 kilometers.

Pluto.Text: Pluto is a dwarf planet in the Kuiper belt, a ring of bodies beyond Neptune. It was the first Kuiper belt object to be discovered and is the largest known plutoid. Pluto has a complex and varied surface, with mountains, plains, and possible ice volcanoes. Pluto's moon Charon is massive and close enough to be considered a double dwarf planet system along with Pluto - they orbit a common barycenter which lies outside of Pluto's surface. In 2015, NASA's New Horizons mission conducted a flyby of Pluto, providing the most detailed images and data of the dwarf planet to date.
Factoids: Pluto has five known moons: Charon, Styx, Nix, Kerberos, and Hydra which all orbit in a complex configuration.
It was reclassified from a planet to a dwarf planet in 2006 upon the discovery of Eris and other similar objects.

Quaoar.Text: Quaoar is a large trans-Neptunian object and possible dwarf planet with one known moon, Weywot.
Factoids: Discovered in 2002.
Named after a creation deity of the Tongva people, native to the Los Angeles area.
Has a relatively bright surface, suggesting it may be covered in water ice.

Rhadamanthus.Text: Rhadamanthus is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

S/2015_M_1 (MK2).Text: S/2015 (MK2) is the only known moon of Makemake.
Factoids: S/2015 (MK2) has a diameter of approximately 175 kilometers.
It was discovered in 2015.

Salacia.Text: Salacia is a large trans-Neptunian object classified as a dwarf planet. It has one known moon, Actaea. Salacia's surface is believed to be composed of a mixture of water ice and rock, giving it a relatively dark appearance compared to other TNOs. Its orbit is moderately eccentric and inclined, taking it through the outer regions of the Solar System.
Factoids: It has one known moon named Actaea.
Discovered in 2004.
Named after the Roman goddess of saltwater.

Sedna.Text: Sedna is a distant trans-Neptunian object with one of the most elongated orbits in the Solar System. It is considered a likely dwarf planet. Sedna's orbit takes it far beyond the Kuiper Belt into the inner Oort Cloud, making it one of the most distant known objects in the Solar System. Its surface is extremely cold and covered in a layer of methane and nitrogen ices.
Factoids: Discovered in 2003.
Has an extremely elongated orbit that takes about 11,400 years to complete.
Named after a goddess of the sea in Inuit mythology.

Sila-Nunam.Text: Sila-Nunam is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Teharonhiawako.Text: Teharonhiawako is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Uni.Text: Unis is a mid-sized Kuiper belt object that orbits the Sun beyond Neptune. It has a relatively circular orbit and is thought to be composed of a mixture of rock and ice. Unis's surface is believed to be covered in a layer of complex organic compounds, giving it a reddish color.
Factoids: Unis has an estimated diameter of around 300 kilometers.
It was discovered in 2004.
Named after the ancient Egyptian goddess of the sky.

Vanth.Text: Vanth is the largest moon of the trans-Neptunian object Orcus.
Factoids: Discovered in 2007
Possibly a captured object due to its differing color and composition compared to Orcus.

Varda.Text: Varda is a trans-Neptunian object cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml set.
Factoids: Cataloged from the current SolSystemDense.xml data set.

Varuna.Text: Varuna is a large Kuiper belt object that is considered a possible dwarf planet. It has a rapid rotation period and an elongated shape, suggesting it may be a contact binary or have a significant elongation due to its spin. Varuna's surface is thought to be composed of water ice and other ices, giving it a relatively bright appearance compared to other Kuiper belt objects.
Factoids: Varuna has a rotation period of approximately 6.34 hours.
It was discovered in 2000.
Named after the Hindu god of water and the celestial ocean.

Weywot.Text: Weywot is the only known moon of Quaoar.
Factoids: Weywot has a diameter of approximately 81 kilometers.
It was discovered in 2007.
Named after a mythological figure associated with the Tongva people.
